neutral: You will be presented with an English sentence {en} and two Hindi translations {a} and {b}. Your task is to judge the overall translation quality of each option. Consider both the accuracy of meaning and the fluency/grammar of the translations. Do not express any preference for formal versus casual style. Choose the translation that best conveys the original meaning and reads fluently in Hindi. Answer with EXACTLY one token: A, B, or Tie.
conversational: You will be presented with an English sentence {en} and two Hindi translations {a} and {b}. Your task is to prioritize natural, casual, spoken/chat-style Hindi. Reward translations that use idiomatic colloquial phrasing, casual pronouns, natural tag-questions, and word order. However, if a translation contains a genuine error such as wrong meaning, broken grammar, mistranslation, or a glitch, it must lose regardless of how casual it sounds. Choose the translation that best fits these criteria. Answer with EXACTLY one token: A, B, or Tie.
